title: Nenana musher Bailey Vitello takes first in Nome-Golovin 200 Sled Dog Race
summary: Nenana musher Bailey Vitello finished first in the inaugural Nome-Golovin 200 Sled Dog Race just before midnight Saturday at 11:35 p.m. The finish came after one day, five hours, and 35 minutes on the trail with a team of 12 dogs. Vitello held a lead throughout the race, arriving in Nome an hour and...

title: 2026 commercial herring fishery opens
summary: The Sitka Sound Sac Roe herring fishery opened on Friday (3-27-26) for the first time this year. In an announcement from the Alaska Department of Fish and Game Friday afternoon (3-27-26), state managers said they opened the fishery to commercial seiners from 12 to 6 p.m. in Crescent Bay. title: AST Death Investigation: Lake George [AK26027257]
summary: On March 27, 2026, the Alaska State Troopers were notified that 63-year-old California resident Eric Sommer had been declared deceased at an Anchorage area hospital. A preliminary investigation determined that at around 2:00 pm Sommer had been caught in an avalanche while heliskiing near Lake George. Guides were able to extricate Sommers and transport him to an Anchorage area hospital. Sommers was later pronounced deceased at the hospital. Next of kin have been notified.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: This Day in Alaska History-March 28th, 1898

title: AST DV Assault IV, DV Criminal Mischief V,  False Information or Report: WASILLA [AK26027314]
incident_type: DV Assault IV, DV Criminal Mischief V,  False Information or Report
summary: On March 28th, 2026, at approximately 0210 hours AST, the police received a report of a disturbance in the Wasilla area. AST responded and contacted 40-year-old Natalie Berry, of Wasilla. Investigation revealed that Natalie had assaulted a household member and broken something. Natalie was arrested for DV Assault IV and DV Criminal Mischief V. She was taken into custody and transported to Mat-Su Pretrial without bail.

title: AST Driving in Violation of License Restriction-CMV: Cantwell [AK26027276]
incident_type: Driving in Violation of License Restriction-CMV
summary: On 03/27/2026 at 2144 hours the Alaska State Troopers conducted a traffic stop near Mile 208 Parks Highway on a CMV transporting hazmat for an equipment violation. Investigation determined the driver, Terry Miller, age 69 from Trapper Creek was operating the vehicle without wearing corrective lenses as required by his license. Miller was issued a misdemeanor summons for Driving in Violation of License Restriction.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: New Hunting and Trapping Regulations in Southcentral Alaska
summary: New Hunting and Trapping Regulations in Southcentral Alaska Learn what happened at the SouthCentral Board of Game Meeting. The Southeast game units are highlighted in orange. The Alaska Board of Game (BOG) is responsible for making decisions on wildlife management in Alaska, including population and harvest objectives and hunting regulations, which are then implemented by the Alaska Department of Fish and Game (ADFG). Proposals are submitted by the public, ADFG, and Advisory Committees, and then deliberated upon by the Board, by region on a rotating basis with a three-year cycle (watch our whiteboard video to learn more!). This year, the Board accepted Southcentral proposals . A public comment period was held before the Board deliberation meeting. Alaska Wildlife Alliance attended the Southcentra...
